ocr.text: The first table presents data on various genes, detailing the number of sequences and site model values across several
conditions. For the gene aspat‐3P4, there are 13 sequences, with a site model M1a value of 12.33, BSA of 10.20, BSA1 of 6.70,
and other conditions of 6.37, 0.00*, 5.45, and 5.29. The gene nadpme‐1P4 has 14 sequences, with site model M1a at 10.19,
BSA at 14.19, BSA1 at 9.66, and other values of 13.52, 0.00*, 14.18, and 14.18. The gene ppc‐1P3 has 9 sequences, with site
model M1a at 72.43, BSA at 66.62, BSA1 at 66.58, and additional values of 11.70, 9.85, 5.66, and 0.00*. Lastly, the gene ppdk‐
1P2 has 14 sequences, with site model M1a at 0.00*, BSA at 4.01, BSA1 at 4.01, and other values of 4.01, 4.01, 3.91, and 3.91.
The second table outlines the percentage in the clinical range for various factors. For Factor 1: DIMS, the total sample (n=283)
is 29.6%, girls (n=189) at 30.4%, boys (n=94) at 28.0%, children (n=173) at 23.8%, and teens (n=110) at 38.5%. Factor 2: SBD
shows 6.5% across all demographics, Factor 3: DA is 4.3% overall, varying by gender and age, Factor 4: SWTD is 12.6%,
Factor 5: DOES at 15.9%, Factor 6: SHY at 1.4%, and Total Sleep Problems at 22.0%.
